curriculumData: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders
17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry
21. Public Health, Legal Aspects and Other Related Topics

qnaData.stem: What are the various types of psychotic disorders? How they differ from neurosis? How will you manage a 27-year-old married lady with a diagnosis of paranoid schizophrenia? [2+2+4=8 Marks]

A 72-year-old male was brought by his son with complains of gradual and progressive loss of memory (mainly recent memory), difficulty in doing daily household activities and difficulty in finding the way back home when he is going outside. These symptoms are present for last 7 years. Of late he is also showing irritability and becomes assaultative without any proper reason for last 6 months.
a) What can be his provisional diagnosis?
b) What are your differential diagnosis for his presentations?
c) How will you manage him considering your provisional diagnosis? [1+3+3=7 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on:
a) First Rank Symptoms of Schizophrenia [2 Marks]
b) Define delusions and enumerate its different types [3 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on the following:
a) Trichotillomania
b) Hypochondriasis [2+3=5 Marks]

SAQ: A 47-year-old man, diagnosed with cirrhosis of liver presents to you with a chronic history of alcohol use. He refuses to accept the fact that his alcohol intake has caused his liver problems and refuses any help to stop his drinking habit.
a) What is the stage of motivation in this man?
b) Briefly describe the stages of motivation in substance use disorders? [1+4=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: SAQ: What is insomnia? Describe sleep hygiene techniques. [3+2=5 Marks]

What are the clinical features of manic disorder? How does mania differ from hypomania? Enumerate the management plan for a patient with mania who claims himself to be God and refuses to take any food and Water. [2+2+4=8 Marks]

A 28-year-old lady is brought with complains of repetitive thought that she might throw her 7 months infant out of the balcony. As a mother she does not actually have any intention for this act. She also repetitively thinks that she is uttering obscene words when she is worshipping god, for which she has started tapping her feet each time she starts worshipping.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) Briefly describe the thought abnormality she has?
c) How will you manage her?
d) What other mental health issues she might develop if not adequately treated now? [1+2+3+1=7 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly:
a) Clinical features of Paranoid Personality Disorder.
b) Intellectual disability. [3+2=5 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on the indications, contraindications and various steps involved in administering Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) in a patient having high suicidal intent. [2+1+2=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Write in brief about the Clinical presentations and management of a child with Autism Spectrum Disorder. [3+2=5 Marks]

SAQ: a) Discuss the Psychological aspects of Diabetes Mellitus. [3 Marks]
b) Name few antipsychotics which can worsen diabetes mellitus in a patient with psychosis. [2 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 21. Public Health, Legal Aspects and Other Related Topics

qnaData.stem:
A 42-year-old male came to you with 7 months history of feeling sadness and not enjoying the previously pleasurable activities, which are present persistently and across almost all situations. He feels that his future is dark and he lost hope in his abilities. He would think himself as a man of no worth and he should not live in this world. He told you about his plans to end his own life.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis and your psychiatric differential diagnoses?
b) Explain how will you manage this case?
c) What will be your long-term plan for management? [3+3+2=8 Marks]
What are the types of anxiety disorder? Discuss the management of Agoraphobia with Panic disorder? [3+4=7 Marks]
SAQ: Discuss the First rank symptoms of Schizophrenia. [5 Marks]
SAQ: Discuss the pharmacological management of opioid dependence. [5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: SAQ: What are the points of description of "speech" in Mental Status Examination? Write the findings of description of "speech" in a patient with Depression. [4+1=5 Marks]
SAQ: Discuss the stages of motivation in the management of substance use disorder. [5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
A person with alcohol dependence syndrome presented to emergency with tremors, agitation, sweating, insomnia symptoms after one day of suddenly stopping his alcohol drinking.
a) Explain how will you do the acute management of this case?
b) What will be your long-term plan for management, including pharmacological and non-pharmacological intervention? [4+4=8 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: Discuss the management of pharmacological and non-pharmacological (Psychological) management of obsessive compulsive disorder. [4+3=7 Marks]
SAQ: Uses of non-invasive brain stimulation in psychiatry [5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Write a note on Anorexia Nervosa [5 Marks]
SAQ: Classical and operant conditioning, and its application in psychiatry [5 Marks]
SAQ: Adverse effects of typical and atypical antipsychotic medications [5 Marks]

A 25-year-old male presented to the psychiatry OPD, brought by his father, with complaints of suspiciousness against one of his neighbours. He claims that his neighbour has planned to kill him. His father reports that there is no truth in his claims, which he is holding for last 5 years. He also claims to clearly hear his own thoughts aloud. Of late, he expressed a desire to die.
a) What are the different thought abnormalities in this patient?
b) What is your provisional diagnosis? What can be the differential diagnosis?
c) Elaborate on the management of this patient. [2+2+4=8 Marks]

A 23-years-old female presented to psychiatry with a 6-year complaint that she is not able to control her doubt that her hands are cleaned enough, even when she is repeatedly cleaning them, often for hours. Of late, she was noted to be withdrawn, rarely socializing, eating and sleeping less.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis?
b) How will you manage the patient?
c) Which other disorders can be comorbidly found in her? [2+3+2=7 Marks]
SAQ: What are the clinical features and pharmacological options to manage alcohol withdrawal syndrome? [3+2=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Enumerate the clinical features and management options of a child with Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder? [3+2=5 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on:
i) Pharmacological management of Brown Sugar dependence.
ii) Clinical features of withdrawal from opioids. [3+2=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: SAQ: What is Bipolar Disorder? How will you approach a patient with acute mania? [2+3=5 Marks]

A 37-year-old male was brought with a 25-day history of feeling low, decreased interaction, loss of sleep, and appetite. He also thinks that he has been a burden to his family.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis for the current presentation?
b) What are the various clinical features of the clinical condition that you diagnosed in him?
c) How will you manage him? [1+2+4=7 Marks]

A 70-year-old hypertensive male was brought by his grandson with a 5-year complaint that he is getting lost on his way back home when going outside alone. He also forgot doing her day-to-day activities like bathing, eating, brushing, which he was doing at ease 5 years back. Of late, he gets irritable when he is repeatedly reminded of his daily activities.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis?
b) What are the different clinical features that explain the disorder?
c) What will be your pharmacological options to manage him?
d) Which non-pharmacological treatment will help him? [1+3+2+2=8 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on:
i) Grades of insight, as assessed in mental status examination.
ii) Difference between Mood and Affect. [3+2=5 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on the First rank symptoms of schizophrenia. [5 Marks]
SAQ: What are the manifestations of obsessive-compulsive personality disorder? How does it differ from obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD)? [3+2=5 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on:
i) Anorexia nervosa.
ii) Management of Erectile Dysfunction in males. [3+2=5 Marks]

35 years old male presented to the psychiatry OPD, brought by his wife in view of unmanageability at home due to irritability and anger outbursts, irrelevant talking characterized by tall claims that he can tell the future, he is also suspicious and fearful that a particular person from his village has told him that he will kill him. He also doubts his wife to have colluded with the enemy. Wife also reports that he takes a substance and has frequently caught him with red eyes when he smokes.
a) Comment on the psychopathology.
b) What is the most likely diagnosis? Which substance is most commonly associated with such a presentation?
c) Elaborate on the management of this patient. [2+2+4=8 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
28 years old female was brought by parents in view of irritability and anger outbursts, repetitive washing such that she has stopped doing daily activities including studies and chores.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis? Which scale can be used to assess it?
b) How will you manage the patient?
c) Discuss other possible related disorders with this condition. [2+3+2=7 Marks]
SAQ: Alzheimer’s Dementia: Clinical features and pharmacological options to manage. [3+2=5 Marks]
SAQ: ADHD: clinical features and management. [3+2=5 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Panic Disorder. [2 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Post-partum psychosis. [3 Marks]
SAQ: How will you diagnose Bipolar Disorder? How will you approach a patient with acute mania with psychotic symptoms? [2+3=5 Marks]

A 70 years old male admitted in the ward developed confusion such that he is unable to recognize family member, does not know where he is and is unable to tell time of the day. He has low potassium levels of 2.5 meq/L and has deranged liver function tests with raised SGOT (350 U/L) and SGPT (145 U/L). There is also history of alcohol use in dependence pattern for many years, with last use before admission (15 days back). Currently, he is unmanageable in ward and is removing iv lines.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis for current presentation and etiology?
b) What drug would be preferred to manage his symptoms?
c) Elaborate the management plan for the patient.
d) Which medication would you prefer as anti-craving agent for alcohol? give explanation for it. [2+1+3+2=8 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
35 years old female has presented to the OPD with complains of fear of heights such that she is unable to climb stairs beyond first floor and avoid using lifts. She describes herself as somebody who worries over trivial issues since childhood and would avoid social gatherings and new experiences. She also reports of taking alprazolam 0.5 mg tablets whenever she is very anxious for last 2-3 years, such that currently she is taking up to 6-8 tablets per day.
a) What is the most likely diagnosis?
b) Which psychological intervention would be preferred for fear of heights? Explain.
c) How will you approach and manage this patient? [2+2+3=7 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Impulse control disorders. [3 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 21. Public Health, Legal Aspects and Other Related Topics

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Write briefly on Clinical symptoms and signs of catatonia. [2 Marks]
SAQ: Clinical features of Obsessive-compulsive Personality Disorder. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Dissociative Disorders. [2 Marks]
SAQ: How will you approach and manage a patient in emergency brought by family for recent suicidal attempt? [5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Write briefly on Anorexia nervosa. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on management of Erectile dysfunction. [2 Marks]

A 27-year-old man presented to you with a complain of recent suicide attempt 2 days back. As per family members, for last 6 months, he was also reported to be muttering to self, gesturing in air, disorganized behaviour, disturbed sleep, unprovoked aggression and referring to some aliens in Mars who have secretly stolen his password which he uses for online banking. He is also sleeping less of late and getting violent when asked to visit a psychiatrist.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) Name a scale/ tool which can be helpful in assessing him.
c) What are the dopamine pathways in human brain, and how can their dysfunction explain the above symptoms?
d) How will you approach to manage him? [1+1+2+3=7 Marks]

What are the clinical features of major depressive disorder? What can be the differential diagnoses of a 42-year-old lady who presents with low mood, apprehension, excessive worry for last 2 months? Enumerate your management option for this lady. [2+3+3=8 Marks]
SAQ: Panic Disorder: Clinical features and management. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Management of a suicidal patient in emergency. [3 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: SAQ: What principles of learning will you apply to teach how to wear pants in a 12-year-old child having intellectual disability? [3 Marks]
VSAQ: Usefulness of Psychiatric Classificatory systems. [2 Marks]
VSAQ: Enumerate various drugs used in acute and long-term management of a patient with mania. [2 Marks]
VSAQ: Write briefly about bedside assessment of mood and affect in mental status examination. [2 Marks]

25 years old male presented to the OPD with body pains, irritability, restlessness, running nose and excessive yawning. He also has icterus, multiple injection marks and scars on his upper limbs. He reports of being given an injection for abdominal pain by a local doctor around 2 years back, which later he started injecting by himself for pain relief.
a) What class of substance is the patient using?
b) What comorbid illnesses may be suspected associated with presenting signs and symptoms?
c) What are diagnostic criteria to establish dependence in this patient?
d) How will you manage the patient? [1+1+2+3=7 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
A 25-years old lady presented to the OPD with complains that she developed a behavior of repeatedly checking locks of doors of his house and of almirah multiple times in a day. She feels that without this, her entire house will be robbed off although she knows that she has properly locked them. These symptoms are for last 2 years. Later, she also developed low mood and lack of interest in her daily activities. She believes that god is punishing her by making her do things repeatedly. She even planned to end her life recently.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) What scale (s) will you apply to assess her symptoms and their severity?
c) Briefly describe her psychopathology.
d) How will you approach to manage her? [1+1+2+4=8 Marks]
SAQ: Clinical features and management of Alcohol withdrawal syndrome. [3 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Clinical features of PTSD. [3 Marks]
SAQ: You are doing internship in the emergency ward at AIIMS Deoghar. Mention two Psychiatric Emergencies you may encounter and discuss how you will approach to manage them. [3 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: VSAQ: Enumerate different clinical presentations of catatonia. [2 Marks]
VSAQ: What are the differences in clinical presentations of bipolar disorder and schizophrenia? [2 Marks]
VSAQ: Write very briefly about Classical conditioning. [2 Marks]

A 36-year-old woman presents to the emergency department with a chief complaint and symptoms consistent with panic disorder (intense fear/anxiety, associated with panic attacks: feeling as if she is going crazy or going to die, chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations, sweating, trembling, and dizziness). The episodes last approximately 15 minutes. The episodes have occurred at least once a day for several months, and there are no clear precipitating factors. The patient spends a great deal of time between attacks worrying about having another attack. She has been to the emergency department several times with the same symptoms, and no physical problems were found. She denies alcohol or drug abuse, and her only medical problem is hypothyroidism. Answer the following questions based on the clinical history you have:
a) What are the differential diagnoses for the condition mentioned above?
b) How will you approach and manage the patient in an acute setting?
c) What will be the long-term management plan for the patient? [2+2+3=7 Marks]

What are the clinical features of major depressive disorder? Name a scale to assess depressive symptoms. What is atypical depression? How will you approach to manage a 32-year-old pregnant lady (first trimester) who currently presents with an episode of severe depression with psychotic symptoms and active suicidal ideations? [2+1+2+3=8 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on Compulsive Sexual Behavior Disorder: clinical features and management. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Enumerate the various cognitive domains to diagnose Neurocognitive disorder. [3 Marks]
SAQ: What are the clinical indications and common side effects of Clozapine? [3 Marks]
VSAQ: Importance of assessing pallor in psychiatric disorders. [2 Marks]
VSAQ: What are the clinical features of Lithium toxicity? [2 Marks]
VSAQ: Name the Perceptual Disorders in First Rank Symptoms of schizophrenia. [2 Marks]
What is anxiety? Describe the different types of anxiety disorders? How will you differentiate Generalized Anxiety Disorder from Panic Disorder? [1+4+3=8 Marks]

49-year-old woman is brought with a complain of feeling constant sadness and decreased interest in activities for the last 1 year along with a loss of adequate sleep and appetite. The husband also mentioned that she would always report that she was unable to do household chores and constantly feels that it is not worth living. 2 years back she had an episode of increased activity, increased talkativeness, and decreased sleep, which lasted for 15 days.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) How will you describe her 'mood and affect' and 'thought' in her current psychological state?
c) How will you manage him considering your provisional diagnosis? [1+3+3=7 Marks]
SAQ: Write a short note on ‘Delusion’. [3 Marks]
SAQ: What are the different types of ‘Hallucinations’? [2 Marks]
SAQ: Bedside assessments of cognition [3 Marks]
SAQ: Major classification of antipsychotics with examples. [2 Marks]

A 17-year-old girl (anxious premorbidly) reported with a complaint of recurrent loss of consciousness for the last 24 hours after having lost her grandmother.
a) What are the probable diagnoses?
b) How will you manage her? [2+3=5 Marks]
Who gave the First Rank Symptoms (FRS)? Describe FRS in brief. [1+4=5 Marks]
What are the various Obsessive-Compulsive and Related Disorders (OCRD)? What are the clinical features of Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)? Briefly describe the management of OCD. [2+3+3=8 Marks]

A 32-year-old man is brought to you with complains of talking to self for last 5 years. He also suspects that his colleague has conspired to kill him by making a pact with the government. He also claims to harbor thoughts which are being inserted by that friend in order to control his actions. He also often claims (not persistent) to be having contact with the president of his country, who will save him.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) What are the probable disorders of thought in this man?
c) Enumerate few pharmacological agents used to treat this condition?
d) What are the common side effects of these pharmacological agents? [1+2+2+2=7 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly: Operant conditioning. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly: Types of memory. [2 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly: Importance of classifying psychiatric disorders. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly: What are the current diagnostic classificatory systems used for classifying mental disorders? [2 Marks]
Write a short note on the clinical features and the etiopathogenesis of Neuroleptic Malignant Syndrome. [5 Marks]

What is suicide? How does it differ from parasuicide or non-suicidal self injury?
b) Which mood stabilizer and antipsychotic has anti-suicidal property? [3+2=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem:
A 34-year-old lady presented with a 10-year history of having multiple physical pain symptoms in multiple body areas. She visited many doctors with minimal benefit, which led to dissatisfaction, and further doctor visits. Multiple diagnostic imaging tests done showed insignificant results. She has been referred to you. Her husband also reported that of late she told of low mood, and expressed an intent to die for the last 2 months.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis? Give reasons
b) How will you approach to manage her?
c) Considering suicidal thoughts, which neuromodulation technique can benefit her? [3+4+1=8 Marks]

A 79-year-old woman is brought with a 5-year history of gradual onset of forgetfulness of recent events, facts, and figures, forgetting her way back home from the nearby bank, and difficulty in recognizing her grandson who came to meet her.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) How will you manage her?
c) What might be the possible etiological factors if she shows additional symptoms of disorientation to time, place, and person, with gross inattention, having transitory visual illusions, following a major gastrointestinal surgery? [1+3+3=7 Marks]
SAQ: Write a short note on Erectile dysfunction. [3 Marks]
SAQ: What are the various dissociative/ conversion disorders? [2 Marks]
SAQ: Write briefly on the Management of insomnia. [3 Marks]
SAQ: Pharmacological management options for opioid use disorder. [2 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
During the elicitation of history of a 27-year-old male, you came to know that he has been committing multiple crimes since his early childhood, and has been booked legally on many occasions. Of late, and also before, he nearly tried to throttle a kid over a petty issue, following which he showed no remorse to his act.
a) What can be his diagnosis? Give reasons.
b) What are the different clinical features that supports his diagnosis? [2+3=5 Marks]
What are the different Eating disorders? What is pica? Differentiate between anorexia nervosa and bulimia nervosa [2+1+2=5 Marks]

A 17-year-old male presented to OPD with complaints of hearing voices unheard by others for two days. On examination, he was oriented to time, place, & person, with tachycardia, icterus, & coarse tremors of his bilateral hands. On further clarifying, he reported drinking country-made liquor (500 ml) for the last four years & daily use for two years. His last drink was three days ago.
a) What is your provisional diagnosis?
b) Enumerate the medical complications he is at risk for.
c) Explain how you will manage him? [1+3+4=8 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
A 19-year-old female presented to the hospital with complaints that she is a goddess. She was also found to have dressed excessively & inappropriately for the situation and, on further evaluation, to have spent excessively on unnecessary items over the last 2 months.
a) What is the likely diagnosis?
b) What findings can be obtained on assessment of her psychopathology?
c) Elaborate on the management of the condition. [1+3+3=7 Marks]
SAQ: Elaborate on the symptom domains in Schizophrenia. Who gave the first rank symptoms of schizophrenia? [4+1=5 Marks]
SAQ: Explain the management of manic episode of bipolar disorder briefly. [5 Marks]

SAQ: A 20-year-old male was brought to the OPD by his parents as they found a psychoactive substance in his clothing. They also noted that he had an increased appetite, increased demands for pocket money & irritability when refused. He preferred to be in his room, isolated, or out of the home with friends. At times, he was observed to have red eyes upon returning home. During the interview, he was noted to be irritable, denying any illness, & said he was brought forcefully by his parents.
a) Which substance is the person using?
b) What stage of motivation is the person currently in for his substance use?
c) Describe the stages of motivation. [1+1+3=5 Marks]
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem:
SAQ: A 70-year-old female presented to the OPD with complaints of wandering away from home, only to be brought back by family members for the last few months. She also fails to recognize family members often & would frequently ask for food despite having eaten on time, saying she had not eaten anything. She has stopped doing chores for the last four years because she frequently makes mistakes.
a) What is the provisional diagnosis?
b) How will you manage the patient? [1+4=5 Marks]

A 19-year-old female was referred from the gynaecology department with complaints of amenorrhea for the last 9-10 months. She was tested to be negative for pregnancy. Her family members reported her to be taking very little food per day, and she would constantly monitor her calorie intake and weight. On examination, she was noted to have a BMI of 14 kg/m² and knuckle calluses (Russell's sign).
a) What is the provisional diagnosis?
b) Enumerate the possible medical complications in this condition.
c) Discuss the assessment of the patient.
d) How will you manage the patient? [1+2+2+3=8 Marks]

A 25-year-old male presented to the hospital with complaints of repetitive counting of his steps while walking. He also complains of having a recurrent imagery of a human face, despite not wanting to, which has been causing distress for the last 4 years.
a) What is the provisional diagnosis?
b) Which scale would you like to apply in this case to assess severity?
c) What psychological comorbidities can he have alongside his provisional diagnosis?
d) How will you manage this case? [1+1+2+3=7 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly:
a) Clinical presentations of ADHD.
b) Clinical presentations in Autism Spectrum Disorder. [3+2=5 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly:
a) Post-partum psychosis.
b) Post-traumatic stress disorder. [2+3=5 Marks]

SAQ: Write briefly on the following:
a) Trichotillomania.
b) Indications of Electro-convulsive Therapy. [3+2=5 Marks]

qnaData.stem:
SAQ: a) Describe briefly operant conditioning in Learning.
b) Define memory and describe its types. [3+2=5 Marks]
Which of the following is correctly matched?
Which of the following is NOT an antipsychotic?
Which of the following is a symptom of schizophrenia as proposed by Schneider?
Which of the following symptom/ sign is seen in atypical depression?
Which statement is true about International Classification of Diseases (ICD)?
options: A publication of American Psychiatric Association.
It is limited to only classifying mental and neurological illnesses.
Substance Use Disorders are placed before mood disorders in ICD-10.
It follows a dimensional approach to classifying mental illness.

qnaData.stem: Which of the following is a Cluster C Personality Disorder?
Which of the following does NOT fall in the Triad as described by Beck for depression?
options: Feeling that there is no help available
A sense of loss of confidence
A sense of loss of all hope
A sense of having lost all worth of self

qnaData.stem: In psychogenic/ dissociative pseudoseizures, which is true?
options: Both primary and secondary gain is present

qnaData.stem: Which of the following statements is true for ADHD?
options: Speech abnormalities are present in all children with ADHD.
Impulsivity and Inattention often persists in adulthood.

qnaData.stem: Which of the following medications is not a stimulant drug in ADHD?
Which of the following section in Indian Penal Code (IPC) deals with Mc Naghten's Rule?
qnaData.chapter: 21. Public Health, Legal Aspects and Other Related Topics

qnaData.stem: Giving a chocolate to a kid so as to encourage him to study is an example of?
Which of the following symptoms is NOT present in Bulimia Nervosa?
options: They tend to binge on food
Body weight may be very low in patients with Bulimia Nervosa

qnaData.stem: Identify the mismatch in the following?
options: Rights of persons with disability act 2016: Certification for mental health disability
Narcotic drugs and psychotropics act 1985: Treatment for substance users
Mental Health Care Act 2017: Punishment for crimes by mentally ill.
Mental Health Review Boards: Involuntary admission for mentally ill
qnaData.chapter: 21. Public Health, Legal Aspects and Other Related Topics

qnaData.stem: Which of the following is not a symptom of catatonia?
Which of the following is an antidepressant?
Which of the following mood stabilizer acts well in both the manic and depressive episodes of a bipolar disorder?
Which of the following statements holds true?
options: Delirium Tremens is a complicated withdrawal state of Alcohol intake.
Delirium Tremens is a simple withdrawal state of Alcohol intake.
Antipsychotics are treatment of choice for Delirium Tremens.
Delirium Tremens is seen both in alcohol and cannabis withdrawal states.
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: Which of the following statement is true?
options: Hallucination is a disorder of thought.
Delusion is a disorder of perception.
Obsession is a disorder of thought.
Over-valued Idea is a disorder of mood.

qnaData.stem: Which of the following is NOT a component of the 5A technique for brief intervention for tobacco?
qnaData.chapter: 15. Substance Use and Related Addictive Disorders

qnaData.stem: Which of the following is true for acute stress reaction/ disorder?
options: All ultimately lead to PTSD later.
It may often happen without any precipitating factor.
Depressive symptoms are the predominant symptoms.

qnaData.stem: Which of the following is a positive symptom of psychosis?
Which of the following statement is true?
options: A panic disorder occurs when the cause of the panic is apparently unknown
A panic attack can occur in claustrophobia and it can be also diagnosed as a Panic Disorder
All panic attacks can qualify for a diagnosis of panic disorder
There occurs para-sympathetic stimulation in the early phase of a panic attack

qnaData.stem: A patient with dementia was finding difficulty to come back home after going to a shop (which he generally goes regularly) which of the following lobes is most likely affected?
In non-suicidal self-injuries:
options: A person has an intent to die
Often preceded by a fatal act of suicide
Death is not the final intent.
qnaData.chapter: 17. Emergency Psychiatry and Somatic Treatments in Psychiatry

qnaData.stem: Which of the following drug can be acutely prescribed to a student to prevent a panic attack in an exam without sedating him/ her?
Which of the following best describes Wernicke's encephalopathy?
options: It consists of delirium, alcohol intoxication and ophthalmoplegia.
It consists of gait disturbances, global confusion and ophthalmoplegia.
It consists of gait disturbances, urinary incontinence and ophthalmoplegia.
It consists of urinary incontinence, dementia and ophthalmoplegia.

qnaData.stem: A 22-year-old male college student presents with a 6-month history of social withdrawal, reduced motivation, and neglect of personal hygiene. He reports hearing voices discussing his actions and believes that the television is sending him special messages. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 35-year-old woman presents with excessive worry and anxiety about various aspects of her life, including work, health, and family, for the past 8 months. She finds it difficult to control the worry and experiences associated symptoms such as restlessness, palpitation, fatigue, and muscle tension. She denies any history of panic attacks or substance use. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 50-year-old woman reports feeling depressed for the past 4 months. She also describes significant weight gain and hypersomnia. Her symptoms are worse during the winter and tend to improve during the summer. What is the most likely diagnosis?
options: Major depressive disorder with melancholic features

qnaData.stem: Pavlov's experiment led to the foundation of?

A 24-year-old woman presents with a 3-month history of feeling sad, hopeless, and losing interest in activities she previously enjoyed. She has difficulty sleeping, low energy, and poor concentration. She denies any history of manic episodes, substance abuse, or medical conditions. She reports no suicidal thoughts but feels life is meaningless. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman is brought to the emergency room by her family. She has been neglecting personal care, staying in her room for days, and responding to unseen stimuli. She is often seen laughing inappropriately and appears emotionally detached. These symptoms have persisted for more than 1 year. Which of the following symptoms is considered a negative symptom of schizophrenia?

A 26-year-old male has episodes of excessive energy, decreased need for sleep, grandiosity, and rapid speech, alternating with periods of feeling very low, fatigued, and hopeless. The mood swings have occurred over the past year without any substance use. Which drug you will prefer to manage the acute episode?
A 34-year-old woman presents with a history of two depressive episodes and one hypomanic episode in the past. She has never had a full manic episode. What is the most likely diagnosis?
